---
title: "08_figures_for_report"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
#--------------------------------------------------
# 1. Load packages and saved objects
#--------------------------------------------------

library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(readr)
library(ggplot2)
library(igraph)
library(ggraph)
library(tidygraph)

dir.create(here("outputs"), showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(here("outputs", "figures"), showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(here("outputs", "tables"), showWarnings = FALSE)

clinical_master <- read_rds(here("data", "interim", "clinical_master.rds"))
expr_t <- read_rds(here("data", "interim", "expression_clean.rds"))
meth_t <- read_rds(here("data", "interim", "methylation_clean.rds"))
merged_clinical_expression <- read_rds(here("data", "interim", "merged_clinical_expression.rds"))
merged_clinical_methylation <- read_rds(here("data", "interim", "merged_clinical_methylation.rds"))
merged_clinical_multiomic <- read_rds(here("data", "interim", "merged_clinical_multiomic.rds"))

x_expression <- read_rds(here("data", "processed", "x_expression.rds"))
x_methylation <- read_rds(here("data", "processed", "x_methylation.rds"))
x_multiomic <- read_rds(here("data", "processed", "x_multiomic.rds"))

y_expression <- read_rds(here("data", "processed", "y_expression.rds"))
y_methylation <- read_rds(here("data", "processed", "y_methylation.rds"))
y_multiomic <- read_rds(here("data", "processed", "y_multiomic.rds"))

report_table <- read_csv(
  here("outputs", "tables", "report_model_table.csv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
)

methylation_rf_varimp <- read_csv(
  here("outputs", "tables", "methylation_rf_top_features.csv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
)
```

```{r}
#--------------------------------------------------
# 2. Figure 1: cohort / sample accounting figure
#--------------------------------------------------

cohort_flow <- tibble(
  stage = factor(
    c(
      "Clinical master",
      "Matched to expression",
      "Matched to methylation",
      "Matched to multiomic",
      "Expression labelled",
      "Methylation labelled",
      "Multiomic labelled"
    ),
    levels = c(
      "Clinical master",
      "Matched to expression",
      "Matched to methylation",
      "Matched to multiomic",
      "Expression labelled",
      "Methylation labelled",
      "Multiomic labelled"
    )
  ),
  n = c(
    nrow(clinical_master),
    nrow(merged_clinical_expression),
    nrow(merged_clinical_methylation),
    nrow(merged_clinical_multiomic),
    length(y_expression),
    length(y_methylation),
    length(y_multiomic)
  )
)

p_cohort_flow <- ggplot(cohort_flow, aes(x = stage, y = n)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(aes(label = n), vjust = -0.4, size = 4) +
  labs(
    title = "Sample accounting across the analysis pipeline",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Number of patients"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))

print(p_cohort_flow)

ggsave(
  here("outputs", "figures", "cohort_flow.png"),
  plot = p_cohort_flow,
  width = 9,
  height = 5
)
```

```{r}
#--------------------------------------------------
# 3. Figure 2: feature filtering summary
#--------------------------------------------------

expr_total <- ncol(expr_t) - 2
expr_kept <- ncol(x_expression)

meth_total <- ncol(meth_t) - 2
meth_kept <- ncol(x_methylation)

feature_filtering <- tibble(
  omics = c("Expression", "Expression", "Methylation", "Methylation"),
  stage = c("Initial", "Kept", "Initial", "Kept"),
  n_features = c(expr_total, expr_kept, meth_total, meth_kept)
)

p_filtering <- ggplot(feature_filtering, aes(x = stage, y = n_features, fill = omics)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  geom_text(aes(label = scales::comma(n_features)),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
            vjust = -0.4,
            size = 3.5) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +
  labs(
    title = "Feature reduction from raw omics data to modelling inputs",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Number of features"
  )

print(p_filtering)

ggsave(
  here("outputs", "figures", "feature_filtering_summary.png"),
  plot = p_filtering,
  width = 8,
  height = 5
)
```

```{r}
#--------------------------------------------------
# 4. Figure 3: final model comparison
#--------------------------------------------------

report_table_plot <- report_table %>%
  mutate(
    label = paste(data_source, model, sep = " - "),
    label = factor(label, levels = label[order(auc)])
  )

p_auc <- ggplot(report_table_plot, aes(x = label, y = auc, fill = data_source)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Cross-validated AUC across all modelling strategies",
    x = NULL,
    y = "AUC"
  ) +
  guides(fill = "none")

print(p_auc)

ggsave(
  here("outputs", "figures", "final_auc_comparison.png"),
  plot = p_auc,
  width = 9,
  height = 6
)
```

```{r}
#--------------------------------------------------
# 5. Prepare methylation model data for feature plots
#--------------------------------------------------

model_data_methylation_only <- read_rds(
  here("data", "processed", "model_data_methylation_only.rds")
)

top_meth_features <- methylation_rf_varimp %>%
  slice_head(n = 5) %>%
  pull(feature)

meth_plot_df <- model_data_methylation_only %>%
  select(case_submitter_id, survival_500, all_of(top_meth_features)) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(top_meth_features),
    names_to = "feature",
    values_to = "beta_value"
  ) %>%
  mutate(survival_500 = factor(survival_500, levels = c("No", "Yes")))
```

```{r}
#--------------------------------------------------
# 6. Figure 4: top methylation feature boxplots
#--------------------------------------------------

p_meth_box <- ggplot(meth_plot_df, aes(x = survival_500, y = beta_value, fill = survival_500)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  facet_wrap(~ feature, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Top methylation features by 500-day survival outcome",
    x = "Survived > 500 days",
    y = "Beta value"
  ) +
  guides(fill = "none")

print(p_meth_box)

ggsave(
  here("outputs", "figures", "top_methylation_boxplots.png"),
  plot = p_meth_box,
  width = 10,
  height = 6
)
```

```{r}
#--------------------------------------------------
# 7. Figure 5: top methylation heatmap
#--------------------------------------------------

top_heatmap_features <- methylation_rf_varimp %>%
  slice_head(n = 20) %>%
  pull(feature)

heatmap_df <- model_data_methylation_only %>%
  select(case_submitter_id, survival_500, all_of(top_heatmap_features)) %>%
  arrange(survival_500) %>%
  mutate(sample_order = row_number()) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(top_heatmap_features),
    names_to = "feature",
    values_to = "beta_value"
  ) %>%
  group_by(feature) %>%
  mutate(beta_scaled = as.numeric(scale(beta_value))) %>%
  ungroup()

p_heatmap <- ggplot(heatmap_df, aes(x = sample_order, y = feature, fill = beta_scaled)) +
  geom_tile() +
  labs(
    title = "Heatmap of top methylation features",
    x = "Patients ordered by survival outcome",
    y = "Feature",
    fill = "Scaled\nbeta"
  )

print(p_heatmap)

ggsave(
  here("outputs", "figures", "top_methylation_heatmap.png"),
  plot = p_heatmap,
  width = 10,
  height = 7
)
```

```{r}
#--------------------------------------------------
# 8. Optional: add a small sample annotation table for the heatmap
#--------------------------------------------------

heatmap_annotation <- model_data_methylation_only %>%
  select(case_submitter_id, survival_500) %>%
  arrange(survival_500) %>%
  mutate(sample_order = row_number())

write_csv(
  heatmap_annotation,
  here("outputs", "tables", "top_methylation_heatmap_annotation.csv")
)
```

```{r}
#--------------------------------------------------
# 9. Figure 6: methylation feature importance lollipop plot
#--------------------------------------------------

top_meth_importance <- methylation_rf_varimp %>%
  slice_head(n = 20) %>%
  mutate(feature = factor(feature, levels = rev(feature)))

p_meth_importance <- ggplot(top_meth_importance, aes(x = Overall, y = feature)) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = 0, xend = Overall, y = feature, yend = feature)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  labs(
    title = "Top methylation features from random forest importance",
    x = "Variable importance",
    y = "Feature"
  )

print(p_meth_importance)

ggsave(
  here("outputs", "figures", "methylation_importance_lollipop.png"),
  plot = p_meth_importance,
  width = 9,
  height = 6
)
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)

# Use your methylation RF variable importance table
# Expected columns: feature, Overall

top_meth_plot_df <- methylation_rf_varimp %>%
  arrange(desc(Overall)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 12) %>%
  mutate(
    feature = factor(feature, levels = rev(feature))
  )

p_top_meth_importance <- ggplot(top_meth_plot_df, aes(x = Overall, y = feature)) +
  geom_segment(
    aes(x = 0, xend = Overall, yend = feature),
    linewidth = 3,
    colour = "lightblue",
    lineend = "round"
  ) +
  geom_point(
    size = 3,
    colour = "#FFD166"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Top Methylation Features",
    subtitle = "Random Forest variable importance",
    x = "Importance",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.10))) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) +
  theme(
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),

    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(colour = "#B9C6DA", linewidth = 0.45),

    axis.title.x = element_text(
      colour = "white",
      face = "bold",
      size = 16,
      margin = margin(t = 10)
    ),
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      colour = "#D9E2F2",
      size = 13
    ),
    axis.text.y = element_text(
      colour = "white",
      size = 13,
      face = "bold"
    ),

    plot.title = element_text(
      colour = "white",
      face = "bold",
      size = 30,
      hjust = 0
    ),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(
      colour = "#B9C6DA",
      size = 16,
      hjust = 0,
      margin = margin(b = 12)
    ),

    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    plot.margin = margin(18, 30, 18, 18)
  )

p_top_meth_importance
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)

#-------------------------------
# 1. Prepare top methylation features
#-------------------------------
top_meth_plot_df <- methylation_rf_varimp %>%
  arrange(desc(Overall)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 15) %>%
  mutate(
    feature = feature,
    y = rev(seq_len(n()))
  )

#-------------------------------
# 2. Build tiny segments to mimic a gradient
#-------------------------------
n_segments <- 120

gradient_df <- map_dfr(seq_len(nrow(top_meth_plot_df)), function(i) {
  
  x_max <- top_meth_plot_df$Overall[i]
  
  tibble(
    feature = top_meth_plot_df$feature[i],
    y = top_meth_plot_df$y[i],
    x_start = seq(0, x_max, length.out = n_segments + 1)[1:n_segments],
    x_end   = seq(0, x_max, length.out = n_segments + 1)[2:(n_segments + 1)],
    seg_id  = 1:n_segments
  )
})

# gold gradient palette: darker -> lighter
gold_gradient <- colorRampPalette(c("#8C5A08", "#C88412", "#F6D36B"))(n_segments)

gradient_df <- gradient_df %>%
  mutate(seg_col = gold_gradient[seg_id])

#-------------------------------
# 3. Plot
#-------------------------------
p_top_meth_importance_gradient <- ggplot() +
  
  # gradient importance bars
  geom_segment(
    data = gradient_df,
    aes(x = x_start, xend = x_end, y = y, yend = y, colour = seg_col),
    linewidth = 3,
    lineend = "round",
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  
  # end points
  geom_point(
    data = top_meth_plot_df,
    aes(x = Overall, y = y),
    size = 4,
    colour = "#F6D36B"
  ) +
  
  # labels on left
  geom_text(
    data = top_meth_plot_df,
    aes(x = -0.01, y = y, label = feature),
    hjust = 1,
    colour = "black",
    fontface = "bold",
    size = 7
  ) +
  
  scale_colour_identity() +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(-0.02, max(top_meth_plot_df$Overall) * 1.12),
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = top_meth_plot_df$y,
    labels = NULL,
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.04, 0.04))
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Top Methylation Features",
    subtitle = "Random Forest variable importance",
    x = "Importance",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) +
  theme(
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    legend.key = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
    
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(colour = "#B9C6DA", linewidth = 0.45),
    
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    
    axis.title.x = element_text(
      colour = "white",
      face = "bold",
      size = 18
    ),
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      colour = "#D9E2F2",
      size = 14
    ),
    
    plot.title = element_text(
      colour = "white",
      face = "bold",
      size = 34,
      hjust = 0
    ),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(
      colour = "#B9C6DA",
      size = 18,
      hjust = 0
    ),
    
    plot.margin = margin(20, 30, 20, 80)
  )

p_top_meth_importance_gradient

ggsave(
  here("outputs", "figures", "methylation_importance_lollipop_transparent.png"),
  plot = p_top_meth_importance_gradient,
  width = 9,
  height = 6)
```


```{r}
#--------------------------------------------------
# 10. Figure 7: top methylation feature network
#--------------------------------------------------

# prepare top features
top_network_features <- methylation_rf_varimp %>%
  slice_head(n = 15) %>%
  pull(feature)

network_df <- model_data_methylation_only %>%
  select(survival_500, all_of(top_network_features)) %>%
  mutate(
    survival_binary = ifelse(survival_500 == "Yes", 1, 0)
  )

# feature-feature correlations
feature_cor_mat <- network_df %>%
  select(all_of(top_network_features)) %>%
  cor(method = "spearman", use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

feature_edges <- as.data.frame(as.table(feature_cor_mat), stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>%
  as_tibble() %>%
  setNames(c("from", "to", "corr")) %>%
  filter(from != to) %>%
  mutate(abs_corr = abs(corr)) %>%
  filter(abs_corr >= 0.35)

# remove duplicate undirected edges
feature_edges <- feature_edges %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(pair = paste(sort(c(from, to)), collapse = "__")) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  distinct(pair, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  select(-pair)

# feature-outcome correlations
outcome_edges <- tibble(
  feature = top_network_features,
  corr = sapply(top_network_features, function(f) {
    suppressWarnings(
      cor(
        network_df[[f]],
        network_df$survival_binary,
        method = "spearman",
        use = "pairwise.complete.obs"
      )
    )
  })
) %>%
  mutate(
    abs_corr = abs(corr)
  ) %>%
  filter(abs_corr >= 0.15) %>%
  transmute(
    from = feature,
    to = "Survival_500",
    corr = corr,
    abs_corr = abs_corr
  )

edge_df <- bind_rows(feature_edges, outcome_edges)

# node table
node_df <- tibble(
  name = c(top_network_features, "Survival_500")
) %>%
  left_join(
    methylation_rf_varimp %>%
      select(feature, Overall),
    by = c("name" = "feature")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    node_type = ifelse(name == "Survival_500", "Outcome", "Feature"),
    Overall = ifelse(is.na(Overall), max(Overall, na.rm = TRUE), Overall)
  )

# build graph
graph_obj <- tbl_graph(
  nodes = node_df,
  edges = edge_df,
  directed = FALSE
)

p_feature_network <- ggraph(graph_obj, layout = "fr") +
  geom_edge_link(aes(width = abs_corr, alpha = abs_corr), show.legend = TRUE) +
  geom_node_point(aes(size = Overall, shape = node_type)) +
  geom_node_text(aes(label = name), repel = TRUE, size = 3) +
  labs(
    title = "Correlation network of top methylation features and survival",
    edge_width = "Absolute\ncorrelation",
    edge_alpha = "Absolute\ncorrelation",
    size = "RF importance",
    shape = "Node type"
  ) +
  theme_void()

print(p_feature_network)

ggsave(
  here("outputs", "figures", "methylation_feature_network.png"),
  plot = p_feature_network,
  width = 10,
  height = 8
)
```

```{r}
#--------------------------------------------------
# 11. Figure 8: feature-to-outcome association network
#--------------------------------------------------

outcome_star_edges <- tibble(
  feature = top_network_features,
  corr = sapply(top_network_features, function(f) {
    suppressWarnings(
      cor(
        network_df[[f]],
        network_df$survival_binary,
        method = "spearman",
        use = "pairwise.complete.obs"
      )
    )
  })
) %>%
  mutate(
    abs_corr = abs(corr)
  ) %>%
  transmute(
    from = feature,
    to = "Survival_500",
    corr = corr,
    abs_corr = abs_corr
  )

star_graph <- tbl_graph(
  nodes = node_df,
  edges = outcome_star_edges,
  directed = FALSE
)

p_star_network <- ggraph(star_graph, layout = "fr") +
  geom_edge_link(aes(width = abs_corr, alpha = abs_corr)) +
  geom_node_point(aes(size = Overall, shape = node_type)) +
  geom_node_text(aes(label = name), repel = TRUE, size = 3) +
  labs(
    title = "Associations between top methylation features and survival",
    edge_width = "Absolute\ncorrelation",
    edge_alpha = "Absolute\ncorrelation",
    size = "RF importance",
    shape = "Node type"
  ) +
  theme_void()

print(p_star_network)

ggsave(
  here("outputs", "figures", "methylation_outcome_star_network.png"),
  plot = p_star_network,
  width = 9,
  height = 7
)
```
